forecast-period.text: A high pressure system south of the Bight extends a ridge over SA. The high will move east during today and Saturday ahead of a weakening trough that is expected to brush the west and south on the weekend. The high is forecast to be over the Tasman Sea by Monday, extending a ridge over South Australia through until Tuesday.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Possible morning fog patches about the agricultural area. Isolated light showers about the southern agricultural area, clearing during the evening. Isolated showers and possible thunderstorms developing in the far west. Maximum temperatures below average about the agricultural area but near or slightly above average elsewhere. South to southeasterly winds, tending southeast to northeasterly in the west.

Early morning frost about the southeast. Possible fog patches. Isolated showers and possible thunderstorms extending over the West Coast district, possibly reaching Eyre Peninsula and Kangaroo Island towards midnight. Maximum temperatures below average in the east but above average in the west. Northeast to northwesterly winds, shifting southwest to southerly in the far southwest later.

Isolated showers and possible thunderstorms moving across the agricultural area. Isolated showers persisting about southern coasts into the afternoon. Dry across the north and west. Maximum temperatures slightly above average, tending to near to slightly below average in the far northeast. Northerly winds ahead of a fresh southerly change extending from the west during the day.

text.p: Fine apart from isolated early morning showers about southern coasts. Maximum temperatures slightly above average. Southeast to northeasterly winds.
Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Monday: Up to 2mm for the agricultural area and West Coast district, and up to 2 to 5 mm over southern coasts and ranges and the Lower South East.
